content.problemStatement:
Binary search is an efficient algorithm for finding a specific target value within a sorted array or list. It basically uses the ordered nature of the array to its advantage to find an element in an array very fast.

Binary search works as -


Initialise the search space with two indices, leftleftleft and rightrightright. Initially, left is set to the beginning of the array and right is set to the end.
Take the mid-point of the search space as mid=(left+right)/2mid = (left + right)/2mid=(left+right)/2.
Compare the target element to the element at the midmidmid index.

If the target value matches the midpoint value, the search is successful.
If the target value is less than the midpoint value, update right=mid−1right = mid - 1right=mid−1 to search the left half.
If the target value is greater than the midpoint value, update left=mid+1left = mid + 1left=mid+1 to search the right half.


Repeat till the search space is not empty, i.e, right>=leftright >= leftright>=left.
TaskThe binary search function searches for the index of the element, returns back the index (1-base) if the element is found, else returns -1.
Complete the implementation of the binary search function to solve this problem.
